\"\"\"\n101\t    to_encode = payload.copy()\n102\t\n103\t    # Refresh tokens last 7 days\n104\t    expire = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=JWT_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_DAYS)\n105\t\n106\t )\n178\t\n179\t\n180\tdef refresh_access_token(refresh_token: str) -> str:\n181\t    \"\"\"\n182\t    Generate new access token from valid refresh token\n183\t\n184\t    Args:\n185\t Args:\n219\t        jti: JWT ID to revoke\n220\t        ttl: Time to live in seconds (default: 7 days)\n221\t    \"\"\"\n222\t    if not _redis_client:\n223\t        logger.warning(\"Token revocation attempted but Redis not available\")\n224\t        return\n225\t\n226\t    if ttl is None:\n227\t        ttl = JWT_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_DAYS * 24 * 60 * 60  # 7 days in seconds\n228\t\n229\t logger.error(f\"Failed to revoke token: {e}\")\n234\t\n235\t\n236\tdef get_user_tier_from_token(token_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> UserTier:\n237\t    \"\"\"\n238\t    Extract user tier from token \"\"\"\n266\t    try:\n267\t        tier_enum = UserTier(tier)\n268\t        limits = TIER_CONFIGURATIONS[tier_enum]\n269\t\n270\t        return {\n271\t            \"rate_limit\": }\n286\t\n287\t\n288\tdef get_rate_limit_for_tier(tier: str) -> str:\n289\t    \"\"\"\n290\t    Get rate limit string for a tier\n291\t\n292\t    Args:\n293\t        tier: Tier name\n294\t\n295\t get_user_tier(request: Request) -> str:\n303\t    \"\"\"\n304\t    Get user tier from request (with graceful fallback to free tier)\n305\t\n306\t    Args:\n307\t        request: FastAPI request object\n308\t\n309\t    Returns:\n310\t        Tier string (free/premium/enterprise)\n311\t    \"\"\"\n312\t    # Extract Authorization header\n313\t    auth_header = request.headers.get(\"Authorization\", \"\")\n314\t\n315\t    # No header or empty = free tier\n316\t    if not auth_header or not auth_header.strip():\n317\t        return UserTier.FREE.value\n318\t\n319\t    # Parse Bearer token\n320\t    parts = auth_header.split()\n321\t    if len(parts) != 2 or parts[0].lower() != \"bearer\":\n322\t        logger.debug(\"Invalid authorization header format\")\n323\t        return UserTier.FREE.value\n324\t\n325\t    token = parts[1]\n326\t    if not token or not token.strip():\n327\t        return UserTier.FREE.value\n328\t\n329\t    # Try to validate token\n330\t    try:\n331\t        payload = validate_token(token)\n332\t        tier = get_user_tier_from_token(payload)\n333\t        return tier.value\n334\t    except HTTPException:\n335\t        # Invalid/expired token = gracefully fall back to free tier\n336\t        logger.debug(\"Token validation failed, falling back to free tier\")\n337\t        return UserTier.FREE.value\n338\t\n339\t\n340\tdef get_user_tier_with_fallback(request: Request) -> str:\n341\t    \"\"\"\n342\t    Get user tier with explicit fallback handling (alias for get_user_tier)\n343\t\n344\t    Args:\n345\t        request: FastAPI request object\n346\t\n347\t    Returns:\n348\t        Tier string, never raises exception\n349\t    \"\"\"\n350\t    return get_user_tier(request)\n351\t\n352\t\n353\t# Advanced security features\n354\t\n355\tdef create_access_token_with_binding(\n356\t    payload: Dict[str, Any],\n357\t    user_agent: str,\n358\t    expires_delta: Optional[timedelta] = None\n359\t) -> str:\n360\t    \"\"\"\n361\t    Create token bound to user agent (session hijacking prevention)\n362\t\n363\t    Args:\n364\t        payload: Token payload\n365\t        user_agent: User agent string from request\n366\t        expires_delta: Custom expiration\n367\t\n368\t    Returns:\n369\t        Token with user agent hash binding\n370\t    \"\"\"\n371\t    # Hash user agent for privacy\n372\t    ua_hash = hashlib.sha256(user_agent.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]\n373\t\n374\t    payload_with_binding = payload.copy()\n375\t    async def semantic_compare(\n355\t        self,\n356\t        text1: str,\n357\t        text2: str,\n358\t        api_key: str,\n359\t    ) -> str:\n360\t        \"\"\"\n361\t        Use \"Compare these two facts about a user. Return ONLY one word:\\n\"\n366\t            \"- 'duplicate' if they express the same information\\n\"\n367\t            \"- 'contradiction' if they directly contradict each other\\n\"\n368\t            \"- 'refinement' if Fact 2 is a more specific or updated version of Fact 1\\n\"\n369\t            \"- 'distinct' if they are unrelated or independently useful\\n\\n\"\n370\t            f\"Fact 1: {text1}\\nFact 2: {text2}\"\n371\t        )\n372\t        try:\n373\t            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n374\t                resp = await client.post(\n375\t              if result in (\"duplicate\", \"contradiction\", \"refinement\", \"distinct\"):\n391\t                    return result\n392\t                return \"distinct\"\n393\t        except Exception:\n394\t            return \"distinct\"  # Fallback to safe default\n395\t\n396\t    def _similarity_ratio(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> float:\n397\t        \"\"\"Calculate simple Jaccard similarity between two strings.\"\"\"\n398\t        if not s1 or not s2:\n399\t            return 0.0\n400\t        words1 = set(s1.split())\n401\t        words2 = set(s2.split())\n402\t _is_private_ip(ip_str: str) -> bool:\n811\t        \"\"\"Check if an IP address is private/internal (SSRF protection).\"\"\"\n812\t        try:\n813\t            addr = ip_address(ip_str)\n814\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d79350e5b1314ac791f5c085","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# DUCK-E: The Duck That Talks Back\n2\t\n3\t**DUCK-E** (Digitally Unified Conversational Knowledge Engine) is an AI-powered voice assistant inspired by rubber duck debugging. Instead of explaining your problem to a silent rubber duck, DUCK-E listens and talks back — asking questions, offering suggestions, and searching the web in real time.\n4\t\n5\t## How it works\n6\t\n7\t```\n8\tBrowser (WebRTC) → WebSocket → FastAPI → OpenAI Realtime API\n9\t                                              (gpt-4o-realtime-preview)\n10\t```\n11\t\n12\t1. Your browser captures audio via the MediaDevices API and opens a WebRTC peer connection.\n13\t2. The FastAPI backend requests an ephemeral key from OpenAI — your real API key never touches the browser.\n14\t3. Speech is transcribed by OpenAI Whisper-1; the Realtime API handles both understanding and voice response natively.\n15\t4. When DUCK-E needs external data (weather, web search), it calls the appropriate tool on the server and folds the result into its reply.\n16\t\n17\t## Features\n18\t\n19\t### Voice I/O\n20\t- Low-latency, full-duplex voice conversation via the OpenAI Realtime API\n21\t- 11 built-in voices — changeable mid-session without reconnecting\n22\t- Interruption-friendly: DUCK-E handles natural conversation flow\n23\t\n24\t### Tools\n25\t| Tool | What it does |\n26\t|------|-------------|\n27\t| `get_current_weather` | Current conditions via Open-Meteo (free, no key required) |\n28\t| `get_weather_forecast` | Multi-day forecast via Open-Meteo |\n29\t| `web_search` | Live web search via OpenAI's `gpt-4o-mini` + web_search_preview |\n30\t| `web_fetch` | Fetches and parses a URL (SSRF-protected; blocks private IPs) |\n31\t| `save_memory` / `recall_memories` | Stores and retrieves facts about the user |\n32\t| `change_voice` | Switches voice mid-session |\n33\t\n34\t### Persistent memory\n35\tDUCK-E stores per-user facts with categories, confidence scores, and time decay, and surfaces them at the start of each session. Memory is keyed by user identity via:\n36\t- **Google OAuth** (recommended): Users sign in with Google, memory is keyed to their email\n37\t- **Reverse proxy headers**: Falls back to `x-forwarded-user` / `x-forwarded-email` headers if OAuth is not configured\n38\t\n39\tMemory is silently disabled in local dev when neither authentication method is available.\n40\t\n41\t### Cost protection\n42\t- Per-session spend cap (default: $5)\n43\t- Hourly spend cap (default: $50)\n44\t- Circuit-breaker threshold (default: $100 — disables new sessions for 30 minutes)\n45\t- Maximum session duration (default: 30 minutes)\n46\t- Client-side live cost display derived from token counts in `response.done` events\n47\t\n48\t### Security\n49\t- Ephemeral key flow — real `OPENAI_API_KEY` never sent to the browser\n50\t- Per-IP rate limiting via slowapi\n51\t- SSRF protection on `web_fetch` (resolves hostnames, rejects private/loopback ranges)\n52\t- Input validation via Pydantic models (`LocationInput`, `SearchQuery`, `FetchUrl`)\n53\t- CORS origin whitelist\n54\t- Security headers middleware (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.)\n55\t\n56\t## Quick start\n57\t\n58\t### Docker (fastest)\n59\t\n60\t```bash\n61\tdocker run -d \\\n62\t  -p 8000:8000 \\\n63\t  -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... \\\n64\t  ghcr.io/jedarden/duck-e:latest\n65\t```\n66\t\n67\tOpen `http://localhost:8000` and start talking.\n68\t\n69\t### Docker Compose\n70\t\n71\tCreate a `.env` file:\n72\t\n73\t```\n74\tOPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...\n75\t```\n76\t\n77\tThen run:\n78\t\n79\t```bash\n80\tdocker-compose up -d\n81\t```\n82\t\n83\t### Local development\n84\t\n85\t```bash\n86\tpip install -r requirements.txt\n87\t# create .env with OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...\n88\tuvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n89\t```\n90\t\n91\t## Environment variables\n92\t\n93\t| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n94\t|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n95\t| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | Yes | — | Must have Realtime API access |\n96\t| `REALTIME_MODEL` | No | `gpt-4o-realtime-preview` | Override the realtime model |\n97\t| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` | No | — | Google OAuth client ID (for user authentication) |\n98\t| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` | No | — | Google OAuth client secret |\n99\t| `GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI` | No | `http://localhost:8000/auth/callback` | OAuth callback URL |\n100\t| `JWT_SECRET_KEY` | No | `your-secret-key-here-change-in-production` | JWT signing secret |\n101\t| `JWT_ALGORITHM` | No | `HS256` | JWT algorithm |\n102\t| `JWT_EXPIRATION_MINUTES` | No | `120` | JWT token expiration time |\n103\t| `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED` | No | `true` | Toggle per-IP rate limiting |\n104\t| `RATE_LIMIT_WEBSOCKET` | No | `5/minute` | Per-IP WebSocket connection rate |\n105\t| `COST_PROTECTION_ENABLED` | No | `true` | Toggle cost protection |\n106\t| `COST_PROTECTION_MAX_SESSION_COST_USD` | No | `5.0` | Per-session spend cap |\n107\t| `COST_PROTECTION_MAX_TOTAL_COST_PER_HOUR_USD` | No | `50.0` | Hourly spend cap |\n108\t| `COST_PROTECTION_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD_USD` | No | `100.0` | Kill-switch threshold |\n109\t| `COST_PROTECTION_MAX_SESSION_DURATION_MINUTES` | No | `30` | Maximum session length |\n110\t| `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` | No | — | CORS origin whitelist (comma-separated) |\n111\t| `GRAFANA_PASSWORD` | No | — | For the hardened docker-compose stack |\n112\t\n113\t## API endpoints\n114\t\n115\t| Method | Path | Description |\n116\t|--------|------|-------------|\n117\t| `GET` | `/` | Main chat UI |\n118\t| `WS` | `/session` | Real-time audio WebSocket |\n119\t| `GET` | `/status` | Health check + version |\n120\t| `GET` | `/health/openai` | Tests ephemeral key creation |\n121\t| `GET` | `/metrics` | Prometheus metrics |\n122\t| `GET` | `/auth/login` | Initiate Google OAuth flow |\n123\t| `GET` | `/auth/callback` | OAuth callback handler |\n124\t| `GET` | `/auth/config` | Check OAuth configuration |\n125\t| `GET` | `/auth/me` | Validate JWT and get user info |\n126\t\n127\t## Voices\n128\t\n129\tDUCK-E ships with 11 voices from the OpenAI Realtime API. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_41abeda42b294872810d5b81","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# DUCK-E - Coding Environment\n2\t\n3\t## Project Overview\n4\t\n5\tDUCK-E is a real-time voice assistant built on FastAPI + OpenAI Realtime API. It provides voice-first interaction with tools for weather, web search, web fetch, memory, and voice changing.\n6\t\n7\t## Architecture\n8\t\n9\t```\n10\tBrowser (WebRTC) → WebSocket → FastAPI → OpenAI Realtime API\n11\t                                      ↓\n12\t                                 Tools (weather/search/memory/fetch)\n13\t```\n14\t\n15\t### Key Components\n16\t\n17\t| File | Purpose |\n18\t|------|---------|\n19\t| `app/main.py` | FastAPI app, WebSocket endpoint `/session`, tool handlers |\n20\t| `app/realtime_session.py` | Custom RealtimeSession (replaced AG2 RealtimeAgent) |\n21\t| `app/memory.py` | UserMemoryStore for per-user facts (JSON file store) |\n22\t| `app/config.py` | OpenAI config generation (`get_realtime_config()`) |\n23\t| `app/website_files/static/main.js` | Frontend: WebRTC, cost tracking, transcript rendering |\n24\t| `app/website_files/static/ag2client.js` | WebRTC+WebSocket bridge (originally AG2, now compatible) |\n25\t| `app/middleware/` | Rate limiting, cost protection, security headers |\n26\t\n27\t## Development\n28\t\n29\t### Running locally\n30\t\n31\t```bash\n32\t# Install deps\n33\tpip install -r requirements.txt\n34\t\n35\t# Set required env vars in .env:\n36\t# OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...\n37\t# WEATHER_API_KEY=...\n38\t\n39\t# Run dev server\n40\tuvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000\n41\t```\n42\t\n43\t### Docker\n44\t\n45\t```bash\n46\tdocker build -t duck-e:latest .\n47\tdocker run -p 8000:8000 -e OPENAI_API_KEY=... -e WEATHER_API_KEY=... duck-e:latest\n48\t```\n49\t\n50\t## Important Patterns\n51\t\n52\t### Tool Registration\n53\t\n54\tTools are registered in `main.py` via `session.register_tool()`:\n55\t\n56\t```python\n57\tsession.register_tool(\n58\t    name=\"get_current_weather\",\n59\t    description=\"Get current weather for a location\",\n60\t    handler=get_current_weather,\n61\t    parameters={...}  # OpenAI JSON schema\n62\t)\n63\t```\n64\t\n65\t### WebSocket Message Flow\n66\t\n67\t1. Client connects → `RealtimeSession.run()` accepts WebSocket\n68\t2. Server fetches ephemeral key from `/v1/realtime/sessions`\n69\t3. Server sends `ag2.init` message with config (ephemeral key only, never real API key)\n70\t4. Client establishes WebRTC data channel\n71\t5. Tool calls flow: `response.function_call_arguments.done` → execute → `conversation.item.create`\n72\t\n73\t### Cost Tracking\n74\t\n75\t- Server-side: `CostProtectionMiddleware` limits per-session spend ($5 default)\n76\t- Client-side: `main.js` accumulates usage from `response.done` events, displays hourly estimate\n77\t- Backend API calls (web_search via OpenAI Responses API) tracked separately\n78\t\n79\t### Memory System\n80\t\n81\t- User identity from headers: `x-forwarded-user`, `x-forwarded-email`\n82\t- JSON file store: `/data/memory/{user_hash}.json`\n83\t- Tools: `save_memory(fact)`, `recall_memories()`\n84\t- Memories injected into system message at session start\n85\t\n86\t### Security\n87\t\n88\t- **SSRF protection**: `FetchUrl` validator resolves hostnames, blocks private IPs\n89\t- **Rate limiting**: Per-IP limits via `slowapi`\n90\t- **Input validation**: `LocationInput`, `SearchQuery`, `FetchUrl` validators\n91\t- **CORS**: Origin whitelist in `app.middleware.configure_cors()`\n92\t- **Cost protection**: $5/session hard limit, $100 circuit breaker\n93\t\n94\t## Model Configuration\n95\t\n96\tUses `gpt-realtime-2` for voice sessions. Config generated in `app/config.py`:\n97\t\n98\t```python\n99\t{\n100\t    \"model\": \"gpt-realtime-2\",\n101\t    \"api_key\": os.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\"),\n102\t    \"voice\": \"alloy\",  # changeable via tool\n103\t}\n104\t```\n105\t\n106\t## Testing\n107\t\n108\t```bash\n109\t# Install dev dependencies\n110\tpip install -r requirements-dev.txt\n111\t\n112\t# Run tests\n113\tpytest\n114\t\n115\t# Security tests (92% coverage)\n116\tpytest tests/security/\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\t## Deployment\n120\t\n121\t- **CI/CD**: ArgoCD WorkflowTemplate `duck-e-build` in iad-ci cluster → Docker image → `ghcr.io/jedarden/duck-e`\n122\t- **Production cluster**: apexalgo-iad (accessed via kubectl-proxy over Tailscale)\n123\t- **Auth**: Behind oauth2-proxy or Tailscale auth for header injection\n124\t- **Metrics**: Prometheus metrics at `/metrics`\n125\t\n126\t## Common Gotchas\n127\t\n128\t1. **Ephemeral key flow**: Never send real `OPENAI_API_KEY` to client. Only send `client_secret.value` from `/v1/realtime/sessions` response.\n129\t2. **Voice change**: Requires session reinit — `change_voice` tool sends new config, client reconnects WebRTC.\n130\t3. **Memory persistence**: Requires user headers from reverse proxy — won't work in local dev without mocking headers.\n131\t4. **AG2 references**: Code still uses `ag2.init` message type for client compatibility, but backend is custom `RealtimeSession`.\n132\t5. **Cost display resets**: On reconnect, `resetCostState()` called in `main.js` → `connectAudio()`.\n133\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_b3366e37ce31499c964720c8","session_id":"b72ed55c-c809-4a30-a036-5c859c11775e","uuid":"ccbae9f4-f948-4d7d-92c7-acacea8af13f","timestamp":"2026-06-25T04:56:37.912Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore authentication and memory code"}

Current Authentication Mechanism\n\n**Multi-layered Authentication System:**\n\nThe system supports **three authentication methods** with graceful fallback:\n\n1. **Google OAuth 2.0** (Primary method)\n   - Location: `/home/coding/duck-e/app/middleware/google_oauth.py` (500 lines)\n   - Full OAuth 2.0 flow implementation with Google\n   - Environment variables: `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI`\n   - JWT token creation with user identity (email as subject)\n   - In-memory state and session storage (can be replaced with Redis)\n   - Automatic cleanup of expired states (10 min) and sessions (7 days)\n   - OAuth users automatically get \"premium\" tier\n\n2. **Reverse Proxy Headers** (Fallback method)\n   - Headers: `x-forwarded-user`, `x-forwarded-email`, `x-forwarded-name`\n   - Used when OAuth is not configured\n   - Designed for deployment behind oauth2-proxy or similar authentication proxies\n   - Logs authentication-relevant headers for debugging\n\n3. **JWT Token Authentication** (Optional)\n   - Location: `/home/coding/duck-e/app/middleware/auth.py` (536 lines)\n   - JWT creation/validation with Redis-based token revocation support\n   - Tier-based access control (free/premium/enterprise)\n   - Token binding to user agent and IP for security\n   - Access tokens (2h default) and refresh tokens (7 days default)\n\n**Current User Identification Flow:**\n```\n1. Check JWT token in Authorization header or query params\n2. If JWT valid → extract user email → premium tier\n3. If no JWT → check x-forwarded-email header\n4. If no proxy headers → anonymous user (free tier)\n```\n\n### 2. Memory System Implementation\n\n**UserMemoryStore Class:**\n- Location: `/home/coding/duck-e/app/memory.py` (602 lines)\n- JSON file-based storage: `/data/memory/{user_hash}.json`\n- User identity hashing for privacy and security\n\n**Key Features:**\n- **Structured facts** with categories: preference, personal, correction, context\n- **Confidence scoring** (0.0-1.0) with time-based decay\n- **Semantic deduplication** using gpt-5.4-nano for intelligent comparison\n- **Auto-extraction** from conversation turns using GPT-4o-nano\n- **Fact limits**: 100 facts per user, oldest trimmed when limit reached\n- **Source tracking**: AUTO (extracted) vs EXPLICIT (user-saved)\n- **Time decay**: Facts lose confidence over 30+ days if not referenced\n\n**Memory Integration in WebSocket Sessions:**\n- User identity determines memory keying\n- Memory store created per authenticated user\n- User summary injected into system message at session start\n- Tools: `save_memory(fact)`, `recall_memories(topic)`\n- Automatic extraction on each conversation turn\n\n**Current User Identity Resolution:**\n```python\n# Priority order:\n1. JWT token email (highest priority)\n2. x-forwarded-email header  \n3. x-forwarded-user header\n4. No identity → memory disabled\n```\n\n### 3. Existing OAuth Configuration\n\n**Google OAuth Implementation:**\n- Complete OAuth 2.0 flow with state parameter validation\n- Token exchange and user info retrieval from Google APIs\n- JWT token generation with user identity\n- Session management with automatic cleanup\n- Security features: state validation, token expiration, CSRF protection\n\n**OAuth Endpoints:**\n- `/auth/login` - Initiates Google OAuth flow\n- `/auth/callback` - Handles OAuth callback from Google\n- `/auth/config` - Returns OAuth configuration status\n- `/auth/me` - Validates JWT token and returns user info\n\n**Environment Configuration:**\n```bash\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your_google_client_id_here\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=your_google_client_secret_here\nGOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI=http://localhost:8000/auth/callback\nJWT_SECRET_KEY=your_jwt_secret_key_here_change_in_production\nJWT_ALGORITHM=HS256\nJWT_EXPIRATION_MINUTES=120\nJWT_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_DAYS=7\n```\n\n### 4. Middleware Configuration\n\n**Current Middleware Stack:**\n1. **SecurityHeadersMiddleware** - OWASP security headers\n2. **CORSConfig** - Cross-origin resource sharing\n3. **CostProtectionMiddleware** - Session cost tracking and limits\n4. **RateLimitMiddleware** - Per-IP rate limiting via slowapi\n5. **WebSocketSecurityMiddleware** - WebSocket origin validation\n\n**Important Note:**\nThe `JWTAuthMiddleware` is defined but **NOT currently active** in the middleware stack. Instead, user identification is handled directly in the WebSocket endpoint (`/session`) through:\n\n```python\n# In main.py WebSocket handler:\n- Manual header extraction (x-forwarded-* headers)\n- JWT token validation via get_user_info_from_token()\n- User identity resolution with priority fallback\n- UserTier-based rate limiting integration\n```\n\n### 5. Key Architecture Observations\n\n**Memory-Authentication Integration:**\n- Memory is **completely dependent** on user identification\n- No user identity = no memory system activation\n- Memory keyed by email address (from JWT or proxy headers)\n- System gracefully degrades when authentication unavailable\n\n**Security Considerations:**\n- Ephemeral key flow for OpenAI API (real key never sent to browser)\n- SSRF protection on web_fetch tool\n- Input validation via Pydantic models\n- WebSocket origin validation before connection acceptance\n- Comprehensive security headers and CSP\n\n**Current Limitations:**\n1. **No active JWT middleware** - authentication handled manually in endpoints\n2. **In-memory OAuth state/storage** - not suitable for multi-instance deployments\n3. **No user account management** - no user CRUD operations\n4. **Manual user identity resolution** - could be centralized in middleware\n\n### 6. can be replaced with Redis)\n35\t_oauth_states: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\n36\t\n37\t# Session storage (in-memory for now - can be replaced with Redis)\n38\t_oauth_sessions: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\n39\t\n40\t\n41\tdef is_oauth_configured() -> bool:\n42\t    \"\"\"Check if Google OAuth is properly configured\"\"\"\n43\t    return bool(GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET)\n44\t\n45\t\n46\tdef generate_state_param() -> str:\n47\t    \"\"\"Generate a secure state parameter for OAuth flow\"\"\"\n48\t    return secrets.token_urlsafe(32)\n49\t\n50\t\n51\tdef store_state(state: str, redirect_uri: str = \"\") -> None:\n52\t    \"\"\"Store OAuth state parameter\"\"\"\n53\t    _oauth_states[state] = {\n54\t        \"redirect_uri\": redirect_uri,\n55\t        \"created_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n56\t    }\n57\t\n58\t\n59\tdef validate_state(state: str) -> bool:\n60\t    \"\"\"\n61\t    Validate OAuth state parameter\n62\t    Returns True if valid, False otherwise\n63\t    \"\"\"\n64\t    if state not in _oauth_states:\n65\t        logger.warning(f\"Invalid OAuth state: {state}\")\n66\t        return False\n67\t\n68\t    state_data = _oauth_states[state]\n69\t    created_at = state_data[\"created_at\"]\n70\t\n71\t    # State expires after 10 minutes\n72\t    if datetime.now(timezone.utc) - created_at > timedelta(minutes=10):\n73\t        logger.warning(f\"Expired OAuth state: {state}\")\n74\t        del _oauth_states[state]\n75\t        return False\n76\t\n77\t    return True\n78\t\n79\t\n80\tdef consume_state(state: str) -> Optional[str]:\n81\t    \"\"\"\n82\t    Consume and return the redirect URI from state\n83\t    Removes state from storage after use\n84\t    \"\"\"\n85\t    if state not in _oauth_states:\n86\t        return None\n87\t\n88\t    redirect_uri = _oauth_states[state][\"redirect_uri\"]\n89\t    del _oauth_states[state]\n90\t    return redirect_uri\n91\t\n92\t\n93\tdef store_session(session_id: str, tokens: Dict[str, Any], user_info: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n94\t    \"\"\"Store OAuth session with tokens and user info\"\"\"\n95\t    _oauth_sessions[session_id] = {\n96\t        \"tokens\": tokens,\n97\t        \"user_info\": user_info,\n98\t        \"created_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n99\t        \"last_accessed\": datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n100\t    }\n101\t\n102\t\n103\tdef get_session(session_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n104\t    \"\"\"Get OAuth session by ID\"\"\"\n105\t    if session_id not in _oauth_sessions:\n106\t        return None\n107\t\n108\t    session = _oauth_sessions[session_id]\n109\t    session[\"last_accessed\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n110\t    return session\n111\t\n112\t\n113\tdef cleanup_expired_states() -> None:\n114\t    \"\"\"Clean up expired OAuth states (older than 10 minutes)\"\"\"\n115\t    cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=10)\n116\t    expired_states = [\n117\t        state for state, data in _oauth_states.items()\n118\t        if data[\"created_at\"] < cutoff\n119\t    ]\n120\t    for state in expired_states:\n121\t        del _oauth_states[state]\n122\t    if expired_states:\n123\t        logger.info(f\"Cleaned up {len(expired_states)} expired OAuth states\")\n124\t\n125\t\n126\tdef cleanup_expired_sessions() -> None:\n127\t    \"\"\"Clean up expired OAuth sessions (older than 7 days)\"\"\"\n128\t    cutoff = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - Please set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables.\"\n151\t        )\n152\t\n153\t    # Use provided redirect_uri or fall back to configured one\n154\t    final_redirect = redirect_uri or GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI\n155\t\n156\t    # Generate state parameter\n157\t    state = state_hint or generate_state_param()\n158\t    store_state(state, redirect_uri=redirect_uri)\n159\t\n160\t    # Build OAuth parameters\n161\t    params = {\n162\t        \"client_id\": GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,\n163\t        \"redirect_uri\": final_redirect,\n164\t        \"response_type\": \"code\",\n165\t        \"scope\": \"openid email profile\",\n166\t        \"state\": state,\n167\t        \"access_type\": \"offline\",  # Get refresh token\n168\t        \"prompt\": \"consent\"  # Force consent to get refresh token\n169\t    }\n170\t\n171\t    # Build authorization URL\n172\t    auth_url = f\"{GOOGLE_AUTHORIZATION_URL}?{urlencode(params)}\"\n173\t\n174\t    return {\n175\t        \"authorization_url\": auth_url,\n176\t        \"state\": state\n177\t    }\n178\t\n179\t\n180\tasync def exchange_code_for_tokens(code: str, redirect_uri: str = \"\") -> Dict[str, Any]:\n181\t    \"\"\"\n182\t    Exchange authorization code for access token\n183\t    Returns dict with access_token, refresh_token, expires_in\n184\t    \"\"\"\n185\t    if not is_oauth_configured():\n186\t        raise HTTPException(\n187\t            status_code=500,\n188\t            detail=\"Google OAuth is not configured\"\n189\t        )\n190\t\n191\t    # Use provided redirect_uri or fall back to configured one\n192\t    final_redirect = redirect_uri or GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI\n193\t\n194\t    # Prepare token request\n195\t    data = {\n196\t        \"code\": code,\n197\t        \"client_id\": GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,\n198\t        \"client_secret\": GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET,\n199\t        \"redirect_uri\": final_redirect,\n200\t        \"grant_type\": \"authorization_code\"\n201\t    }\n202\t\n203\t    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:\n204\t        try:\n205\t            response = await client.post(GOOGLE_TOKEN_URL, data=data)\n206\t            response.raise_for_status()\n207\t\n208\t            token_data = response.json()\n209\t\n210\t            # Return relevant token information\n211\t            return {\n212\t                \"access_token\": token_data.get(\"access_token\"),\n213\t                \"refresh_token\": token_data.get(\"refresh_token\"),\n214\t                \"expires_in\": token_data.get(\"expires_in\", 3600),\n215\t                \"token_type\": token_data.get(\"token_type\", \"Bearer\")\n216\t            }\n217\t\n218\t        except httpx.HTTPError as e:\n219\t            logger.error(f\"Error exchanging code for tokens: {e}\")\n220\t            raise HTTPException(\n221\t                status_code=500,\n222\t                detail=\"Failed to exchange authorization code for tokens\"\n223\t            )\n224\t\n225\t\n226\tasync def get_user_info(access_token: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n227\t    \"\"\"\n228\t    Get user information from Google using access token\n229\t    Returns dict with id, email, name, picture, verified_email\n230\t    \"\"\"\n231\t    if not is_oauth_configured():\n232\t        raise HTTPException(\n233\t            status_code=500,\n234\t            detail=\"Google OAuth is not configured\"\n235\t        )\n236\t\n237\t    headers = {\n238\t        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {access_token}\"\n239\t    }\n240\t\n241\t    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:\n242\t        try:\n243\t            response = await client.get(GOOGLE_USERINFO_URL, headers=headers)\n244\t            response.raise_for_status()\n245\t\n246\t            user_info = response.json()\n247\t\n248\t            # Return relevant user information\n249\t            return {\n250\t                \"id\": user_info.get(\"id\"),\n251\t                \"email\": user_info.get(\"email\"),\n252\t                \"name\": user_info.get(\"name\"),\n253\t                \"given_name\": user_info.get(\"given_name\"),\n254\t                \"family_name\": user_info.get(\"family_name\"),\n255\t                \"picture\": user_info.get(\"picture\"),\n256\t                \"verified_email\": user_info.get(\"verified_email\", False)\n257\t            }\n258\t\n259\t        except httpx.HTTPError as e:\n260\t            logger.error(f\"Error fetching user info: {e}\")\n261\t            raise HTTPException(\n262\t                status_code=500,\n263\t                detail=\"Failed to fetch user information from Google\"\n264\t            )\n265\t\n266\t\n267\tasync def handle_oauth_callback(\n268\t    code: str,\n269\t    state: str,\n270\t    redirect_uri: str = \"\"\n271\t) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n272\t    \"\"\"\n273\t    Handle OAuth callback from Google\n274\t    Returns dict with jwt_tokens and user_info\n275\t    \"\"\"\n276\t    # Validate state parameter\n277\t    if not validate_state(state):\n278\t        raise HTTPException(\n279\t            status_code=400,\n280\t            detail=\"Invalid or expired state parameter\"\n281\t        )\n282\t\n283\t    # Consume state and get redirect URI\n284\t    original_redirect = consume_state(state)\n285\t\n286\t    # Exchange code for tokens\n287\t    tokens = await exchange_code_for_tokens(code, redirect_uri or original_redirect or \"\")\n288\t\n289\t    # Get user information\n290\t    user_info = await get_user_info(tokens[\"access_token\"])\n291\t\n292\t    # Create JWT tokens with user identity\n293\t    jwt_payload = {\n294\t        \"sub\": user_info[\"email\"],  # Subject is the user's email\n295\t        \"tier\": \"premium\",  # OAuth users get premium tier\n296\t        \"name\": user_info.get(\"name\", \"\"),\n297\t        \"email\": user_info[\"email\"],\n298\t        \"picture\": user_info.get(\"picture\", \"\"),\n299\t        \"google_id\": user_info[\"id\"],\n300\t        \"auth_method\": \"google_oauth\"\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    # Create access and refresh tokens\n304\t    access_token = create_access_token(jwt_payload)\n305\t    refresh_token = create_refresh_token(jwt_payload)\n306\t\n307\t    # Store session\n308\t    session_id = jwt_payload[\"sub\"]\n309\t    store_session(session_id, {\n310\t        \"access_token\": tokens[\"access_token\"],\n311\t        \"refresh_token\": tokens.get(\"refresh_token\", \"\"),\n312\t        \"expires_in\": tokens[\"expires_in\"]\n313\t    }, user_info)\n314\t\n315\t    logger.info(f\"User authenticated via Google OAuth: {user_info['email']}\")\n316\t\n317\t    return {\n318\t        \"access_token\": access_token,\n319\t        \"refresh_token\": refresh_token,\n320\t        \"token_type\": \"Bearer\",\n321\t \"\")\nGOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI = os.getenv(\"GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URI\", \"http://localhost:8000/auth/callback\")\nGOOGLE_AUTHORIZATION_URL = \"https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth\"\nGOOGLE_TOKEN_URL = \"https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token\"\nGOOGLE_USERINFO_URL = \"https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo\"\n\n# OAuth State Storage (in-memory for now - can be replaced with \"\"\"Generate a secure state parameter for OAuth flow\"\"\"\n    return secrets.token_urlsafe(32)\n\n\ndef store_state(state: str, redirect_uri: str = \"\") -> None:\n    \"\"\"Store OAuth state parameter\"\"\"\n    _oauth_states[state] = {\n        \"redirect_uri\": redirect_uri,\n        \"created_at\": datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    }\n\n\ndef validate_state(state: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"\n    Validate OAuth state parameter\n    Returns True if valid, False otherwise\n    \"\"\"\n    if state not in _oauth_states:\n        logger.warning(f\"Invalid OAuth state: {state}\")\n        return False\n\n    state_data = _oauth_states[state]\n    created_at = state_data[\"created_at\"]\n\n    # State expires after 10 minutes\n    if datetime.now(timezone.utc) - created_at > timedelta(minutes=10):\n        logger.warning(f\"Expired OAuth state: {state}\")\n        del _oauth_states[state]\n        return False\n\n    return True\n\n\ndef consume_state(state: str) -> Optional[str]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Consume and return the redirect URI from state\n    Removes state from storage after use\n    \"\"\"\n    if state not in _oauth_states:\n        return None\n\n    redirect_uri = _oauth_states[state][\"redirect_uri\"]\n    del _oauth_states[state]\n    return redirect_uri\n\n\ndef store_session(session_id: str, tokens: Dict[str, Any], user_info: Dict[str, Any])   if session_id not in _oauth_sessions:\n        return None\n\n    session = _oauth_sessions[session_id]\n    session[\"last_accessed\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    return session\n\n\ndef get_google_authorization_url(\n    redirect_uri: str = \"\",\n    state_hint: str = \"\"\n) -> Dict[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate Google OAuth authorization URL\n    Returns dict with authorization_url and state parameter\n    \"\"\"\n    if not is_oauth_configured():\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=500,\n            detail=\"Google OAuth is not configured. Generate state parameter\n    state = state_hint or generate_state_param()\n    store_state(state, redirect_uri=redirect_uri)\n\n    # Build OAuth parameters\n    params = {\n        \"client_id\": GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,\n        \"redirect_uri\": final_redirect,\n        \"response_type\": \"code\",\n        \"scope\": \"openid email profile\",\n        \"state\": state,\n        \"access_type\": \"offline\",  # Create JWT tokens with user identity\n    jwt_payload = {\n        \"sub\": user_info[\"email\"],  # Subject is the user's email\n        \"tier\": \"premium\",  # OAuth users get premium tier\n        \"name\": user_info.get(\"name\", \"\"),\n        \"email\": user_info[\"email\"],\n        \"picture\": user_info.get(\"picture\", \"\"),\n        \"google_id\": user_info[\"id\"],\n        \"auth_method\": \"google_oauth\"\n    }\n\n    # Check if this is an OAuth-authenticated token\n        if payload.get(\"auth_method\") != \"google_oauth\":\n            return None\n\n        return {\n            \"email\": payload.get(\"email\"),\n            \"name\": payload.get(\"name\"),\n            \"picture\": payload.get(\"picture\"),\n            \"tier\": payload.get(\"tier\", \"premium\"),\n            \"auth_method\": payload.get(\"auth_method\"),\n            \"google_id\": payload.get(\"google_id\")\n        }\n\n    except jwt.JWTError as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Error decoding JWT token: {e}\")\n        return None\n\n\nasync def initiate_login(request: Request, redirect_uri: str = \"\") -> Response:\n    \"\"\"\n    Initiate Google OAuth login flow\n    Returns redirect to Google authorization page\n    \"\"\"\n    if not is_oauth_configured():\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": \"Google OAuth is not configured\"}\n        )\n\n    # Handle the callback\n        result = await handle_oauth_callback(code, state)\n\n        # For frontend redirect (default behavior)\n        if redirect_to_frontend == \"true\":\n            # Redirect to frontend with access token and user info\n            from urllib.parse import urlencode\n            params = {\n                \"access_token\": result[\"access_token\"],\n                \"user_info\": json.dumps(result[\"user_info\"])\n            }\n            frontend_url = f\"/?{urlencode(params)}\"\n            return RedirectResponse(url=frontend_url, status_code=302)\n\n        # For API usage, return JSON\n        return JSONResponse(content=result)\n\n    except HTTPException as e:\n        if redirect_to_frontend == \"true\":\n            error_url = f\"/?error={e.detail}\"\n            return RedirectResponse(url=error_url, status_code=302)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=e.status_code,\n            content={\"error\": e.detail}\n        )\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Unexpected error in OAuth callback: {e}\")\n        if redirect_to_frontend == \"true\":\n            return RedirectResponse(url=\"/?error=internal_server_error\", status_code=302)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": \"Internal server error\"}\n        )\n\n\ndef get_oauth_login_url(request: Request, redirect_uri: str = \"\") -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Get OAuth login URL (for frontend to initiate login)\n    Returns authorization URL directly without redirecting\n    \"\"\"\n    if not is_oauth_configured():\n        return \"\"\n\n    # pass  # Memory is best-effort; don't crash the session\n127\t\n128\t    def _apply_decay(self) -> bool:\n129\t        \"\"\"\n130\t        Apply time-based confidence decay to all facts.\n131\t        Facts unreferenced for 30+ days lose confidence: auto at 0.1/period, explicit at 0.05/period.\n132\t        Prunes facts whose confidence drops below DECAY_THRESHOLD.\n133\t        Returns True if any facts were pruned.\n134\t        \"\"\"\n135\t        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n136\t        surviving = []\n137\t        for fact in self._facts:\n138\t            try:\n139\t                last_ref = datetime.fromisoformat(fact.last_referenced)\n140\t                if last_ref.tzinfo is None:\n141\t                    last_ref = last_ref.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n142\t max(0.0, fact.confidence - rate * periods)\n150\t\n151\t            if fact.confidence >= DECAY_THRESHOLD:\n152\t                surviving.append(fact)\n153\t\n154\t        pruned = len(surviving) < comparison.\"\"\"\n160\t        return \" \".join(text.lower().split())\n161\t\n162\t    def _is_duplicate(self, new_fact: str, category: FactCategory) -> bool:\n163\t        \"\"\"Check if a fact already exists (exact or near-exact match).\"\"\"\n164\t        new_normalized = self._normalize_text(new_fact)\n165\t        for fact in self._facts:\n166\t            if fact.category == 20 and len(existing_normalized) > 20:\n173\t                    shorter, longer = sorted([new_normalized, existing_normalized], key=len)\n174\t                    if longer.startswith(shorter) or shorter in longer:\n175\t                        return True\n176\t        return False\n177\t\n178\t    def add_fact(\n179\t        self,\n180\t        text: str,\n181\t        category: FactCategory FactSource.EXPLICIT and category == FactCategory.CORRECTION:\n196\t            confidence = 1.0\n197\t\n198\t        # Deduplication: skip if exact or near-exact match exists\n199\t        if lower threshold (more aggressive replacement) since\n204\t        # the user is intentionally correcting information.\n205\t        # CORRECTION category also checks across all categories.\n206\t        new_normalized = self._normalize_text(text)\n207\t        sim_threshold = 0.2 if source == FactSource.EXPLICIT else 0.4\n208\t        categories_to_check = list(FactCategory) if category == FactCategory.CORRECTION else [category]\n209\t\n210\t        facts_to_remove = []\n211\t        for fact in self._facts:\n212\t            if fact.category in categories_to_check:\n213\t                existing_normalized = self._normalize_text(fact.text)\n214\t                if self._similarity_ratio(new_normalized, existing_normalized) >= sim_threshold:\n215\t                    facts_to_remove.append(fact)\n216\t        for fact in facts_to_remove:\n217\t            self._facts.remove(fact)\n218\t\n219\t        # Trim oldest facts if at limit\n220\t        if \"\"\"Return list of fact strings, updating last_referenced timestamps.\"\"\"\n238\t        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n239\t        for fact in self._facts:\n240\t            fact.last_referenced = now\n241\t        return [f.text for f in self._facts]\n242\t\n243\t    def get_structured_facts(self) -> list[StructuredFact]:\n244\t        \"\"\"Return list of structured \"\"\"\n267\t        if not user_text.strip():\n268\t            return\n269\t\n270\t        prompt = (\n271\t            \"Extract facts about the USER worth remembering for future conversations. \"https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions\",\n292\t                    headers={\n293\t                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {api_key}\",\n294\t \"messages\": [\n299\t                            {\"role\": \"system\", \"content\": prompt},\n300\t                            {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": turn},\n301\t             pass\n331\t\n332\t                content = data[\"choices\"][0][\"message\"][\"content\"].strip()\n333\t                facts = json.loads(content)\n334\t                if    async def semantic_compare(\n355\t        self,\n356\t        text1: str,\n357\t        text2: str,\n358\t        api_key: str,\n359\t    ) -> str:\n360\t        \"\"\"\n361\t        Use gpt-5.4-nano to compare two facts semantically.\n362\t        Returns: 'duplicate' | 'contradiction' | 'refinement' | 'distinct'\n363\t        \"\"\"\n364\t        prompt = (\n365\t            \"Compare these two facts about a user. Return ONLY one word:\\n\"\n366\t            \"- 'duplicate' if they express the same information\\n\"\n367\t            \"- 'contradiction' if they directly contradict each other\\n\"\n368\t            \"- 'refinement' if Fact 2 is a more specific or updated version of Fact 1\\n\"\n369\t            \"- 'distinct' if they are unrelated or independently useful\\n\\n\"\n370\t            f\"Fact 1: {text1}\\nFact 2: {text2}\"\n371\t        )\n372\t        try:\n373\t            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n374\t                resp = await client.post(\n375\t                    \"https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions\",\n376\t                    headers={\n377\t                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {api_key}\",\n378\t          )\n387\t                resp.raise_for_status()\n388\t                data = resp.json()\n389\t                result = data[\"choices\"][0][\"message\"][\"content\"].strip().lower()\n390\t                if result in (\"duplicate\", \"contradiction\", \"refinement\", \"distinct\"):\n391\t                    return result\n392\t                return \"distinct\"\n393\t        except similarity between two strings.\"\"\"\n398\t        if not s1 or not s2:\n399\t            return 0.0\n400\t        words1 = set(s1.split())\n401\t        words2 = set(s2.split())\n402\t        intersection = len(words1 & words2)\n403\t        union = len(words1 | words2)\n404\t        return intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0\n405\t\n406\t    def _facts_hash(self) -> str:\n407\t )\n528\t                resp.raise_for_status()\n529\t                data = resp.json()\n530\t            embeddings = [item[\"embedding\"] for item in data[\"data\"]]\n531\t            topic_emb =          norm_a = sum(x * x for x in a) ** 0.5\n537\t                norm_b = sum(x * x for x in b) ** 0.5\n538\t                return dot / (norm_a * norm_b) if norm_a and norm_b else      reverse=True,\n544\t            )\n545\t            return [text for score, text in scored if score >= THRESHOLD]\n546\t        except Exception:\n547\t            return []\n548\t\n549\t = 0.3       # Prune facts below this confidence on load\nDECAY_PERIOD_DAYS = 30      # Days between decay steps\nAUTO_DECAY_RATE = 0.1       # Confidence lost per period for auto-extracted facts\nEXPLICIT_DECAY_RATE = 0.05  # Slower decay for explicitly saved facts\n\n\nclass FactCategory(str, Enum):\n    \"\"\"Categories for user memory facts.\"\"\"\n    PREFERENCE = \"preference\"\n    PERSONAL = \"personal\"\n    CORRECTION = \"correction\"\n    CONTEXT = \"context\"\n\n\nclass FactSource(str, Enum):\n    \"\"\"Source of a memory fact.\"\"\"\n    AUTO = \"auto\"       # Automatically extracted from conversation\n    EXPLICIT = \"explicit\"  # Explicitly saved via save_memory tool\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StructuredFact:\n    \"\"\"A structured memory fact with metadata.\"\"\"\n    text: str\n    category: FactCategory\n    confidence: float\n    source: FactSource\n    created_at: str\n    last_referenced: str\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        return {\n            \"text\": self.text,\n            \"category\": self.category.value,\n            \"confidence\": self.confidence,\n            \"source\": self.source.value,\n            \"created_at\": self.created_at,\n            \"last_referenced\": self.last_referenced,\n        }\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_dict(cls, data: dict) -> \"StructuredFact\":\n        return    created_at=data[\"created_at\"],\n            last_referenced=data[\"last_referenced\"],\n        )\n\n\nclass UserMemoryStore:\n    def __init__(self, user_id: str, memory_dir: str = DEFAULT_MEMORY_DIR):\n        self.user_id = user_id\n        self.memory_dir = Path(memory_dir)\n        # Hash user_id to avoid path traversal and PII in filenames\n        user_hash = hashlib.sha256(user_id.encode()).hexdigest()\n        self.file_path = self.memory_dir / f\"{user_hash}.json\"\n        self._data: dict = {}\n        self._facts: list[StructuredFact] = []\n\n    def load(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Load memory from disk. No-op if file doesn't exist.\"\"\"\n        if self.file_path.exists():\n            try:\n                with open(self.file_path, \"r\") as f:\n                    self._data = json.load(f)\n            except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n                self._data = {}\n        if \"facts\" not in self._data:\n            self._data[\"facts\"] = []\n        if \"user_id\" not in self._data:\n            self._data[\"user_id\"] = self.user_id\n        if \"created_at\" not in self._data:\n            self._data[\"created_at\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n\n f:\n                    # New structured format\n                    self._facts.append(StructuredFact.from_dict(f))\n                else:\n                    # Legacy format - migrate to new format\n                    self._facts.append(StructuredFact(\n                        text=f[\"text\"],\n                        category=FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n                        confidence=0.7,\n                        source=FactSource.AUTO,\n                        created_at=f.get(\"created_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()),\n                        last_referenced=f.get(\"created_at\", datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()),\n                    ))\n\n        # Apply decay and prune stale facts; persist if anything changed\n        if self._apply_decay():\n            self.save()\n\n    def save(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist memory to disk.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            self.memory_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            self._data[\"facts\"] = [f.to_dict() for f in self._facts]\n            self._data[\"updated_at\"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n            with open(self.file_path, \"w\") as f:\n                json.dump(self._data, f, indent=2)\n        except OSError:\n            pass  # Memory is best-effort; don't crash the session\n\n    def _apply_decay(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply time-based confidence decay to all facts.\n        Facts unreferenced for 30+ days lose confidence: auto at 0.1/period, explicit at 0.05/period.\n        Prunes facts whose confidence drops below DECAY_THRESHOLD.\n        Returns True if any facts were pruned.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        surviving = []\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            try:\n                last_ref = datetime.fromisoformat(fact.last_referenced)\n                if last_ref.tzinfo is None:\n                    last_ref = last_ref.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n                days_stale = (now - last_ref).days\n            except (ValueError, TypeError):\n                days_stale = 0\n\n            if days_stale >= DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS:\n                periods = days_stale / DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS\n                rate = EXPLICIT_DECAY_RATE if fact.source == FactSource.EXPLICIT else AUTO_DECAY_RATE\n                fact.confidence = max(0.0, fact.confidence - rate * periods)\n\n            if fact.confidence >= DECAY_THRESHOLD:\n                surviving.append(fact)\n\n        pruned = len(surviving) < len(self._facts)\n        self._facts = surviving\n        return pruned\n\n    def _normalize_text(self, text: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Normalize text for comparison.\"\"\"\n        return \" \".join(text.lower().split())\n\n    def _is_duplicate(self, new_fact: str, category: FactCategory) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if a fact already exists (exact or near-exact match).\"\"\"\n        new_normalized = self._normalize_text(new_fact)\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            if fact.category == category:\n                existing_normalized = self._normalize_text(fact.text)\n                # Check for exact match or one containing the other\n                if new_normalized == existing_normalized:\n                    return True\n                # Near-exact: one is a prefix of the other with significant overlap\n                if len(new_normalized) > 20 and len(existing_normalized) > 20:\n                    shorter, longer = sorted([new_normalized, existing_normalized], key=len)\n                    if longer.startswith(shorter) or shorter in longer:\n                        return True\n        return False\n\n    def add_fact(\n        self,\n        text: str,\n        category: FactCategory = FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n        confidence: float = 0.7,\n        source: FactSource = FactSource.AUTO,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Add a structured fact about the user with deduplication and contradiction detection.\n        Returns True if fact was added, False if duplicate/skipped.\n        Enforces MAX_FACTS limit.\n        \"\"\"\n        text = text.strip()\n        if not text:\n            return False\n\n        # Explicit corrections always override with full confidence\n        if source == FactSource.EXPLICIT and category == FactCategory.CORRECTION:\n            confidence = 1.0\n\n        # Deduplication: skip if exact or near-exact match exists\n        if self._is_duplicate(text, category):\n            return False\n\n        # Contradiction detection: remove same-category facts that likely conflict.\n        # Explicit saves use a lower threshold (more aggressive replacement) since\n        # the user is intentionally correcting information.\n        # CORRECTION category also checks across all categories.\n        new_normalized = self._normalize_text(text)\n        sim_threshold = 0.2 if source == FactSource.EXPLICIT else 0.4\n        categories_to_check = list(FactCategory) if category == FactCategory.CORRECTION else [category]\n\n        facts_to_remove = []\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            if fact.category in categories_to_check:\n                existing_normalized = self._normalize_text(fact.text)\n                if self._similarity_ratio(new_normalized, existing_normalized) >= sim_threshold:\n                    facts_to_remove.append(fact)\n        for fact in facts_to_remove:\n            self._facts.remove(fact)\n\n        # Trim oldest facts if at limit\n        if len(self._facts) >= MAX_FACTS:\n            self._facts = self._facts[-(MAX_FACTS - 1):]\n\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        fact = StructuredFact(\n            text=text,\n            category=category,\n            confidence=confidence,\n            source=source,\n            created_at=now,\n            last_referenced=now,\n        )\n        self._facts.append(fact)\n        self.save()\n        return True\n\n    def get_facts(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of fact strings, updating last_referenced timestamps.\"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            fact.last_referenced = now\n        return [f.text for f in self._facts]\n\n    def get_structured_facts(self) -> list[StructuredFact]:\n        \"\"\"Return list of structured facts with full metadata.\"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            fact.last_referenced = now\n        return self._facts.copy()\n\n    async def extract_and_save(\n        self,\n        user_text: str,\n        assistant_text: str,\n        api_key: str,\n        cost_tracker=None,\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n        on_backend_cost=None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Extract memorable facts from a conversation turn and save them.\n\n        Calls gpt-5.4-nano with a focused extraction prompt to identify user-specific\n        facts worth persisting (location, preferences, interests, personal details).\n        Returns structured facts with category and confidence.\n        Fire-and-forget safe — all errors are suppressed to avoid disrupting the session.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not user_text.strip():\n            return\n\n        prompt = (\n            \"Extract facts about the USER worth remembering for future conversations. \"\n            \"Categorize each fact and assign a confidence score (0.0-1.0).\\n\\n\"\n            \"Categories:\\n\"\n            \"- preference: User's likes, dislikes, choices (e.g., 'prefers dark mode')\\n\"\n            \"- personal: Personal details (e.g., 'lives in Berlin', 'has two cats')\\n\"\n            \"- correction: User correcting previous assistant behavior\\n\"\n            \"- context: Other contextual information (e.g., 'working on Python project')\\n\\n\"\n            \"Confidence scoring:\\n\"\n            \"- 0.9-1.0: Explicitly stated preference or fact\\n\"\n            \"- 0.7-0.9: Reasonably inferred from conversation\\n\\n\"\n            \"Return ONLY a valid JSON array of objects with 'text', 'category', and 'confidence', e.g.\\n\"\n            '[{\"text\": \"User lives in Paris\", \"category\": \"personal\", \"confidence\": 0.9}, '\n            '{\"text\": \"User prefers metric units\", \"category\": \"preference\", \"confidence\": 0.85}]\\n'\n            \"Return [] if nothing is worth saving.\"\n        )\n        turn = f\"[User]: {user_text}\\n[Assistant]: {assistant_text}\"\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.post(\n                    \"https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions\",\n                    headers={\n                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {api_key}\",\n                        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                    },\n                    json={\n                        \"model\": \"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                        \"messages\": [\n                            {\"role\": \"system\", \"content\": prompt},\n                            {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": turn},\n                        ],\n                        \"temperature\": 0,\n                        \"max_tokens\": 512,\n                    },\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                data = resp.json()\n\n                # Track token usage if cost_tracker is provided\n                if cost_tracker is not None and session_id is not None:\n                    usage = data.get(\"usage\", {})\n                )\n                        except Exception:\n                            pass  # Never crash the session over cost tracking\n                        if on_backend_cost is not None:\n usage.get(\"prompt_tokens\", 0),\n                                    usage.get(\"completion_tokens\", 0),\n                                )\n                            except Exception:\n                                pass\n\n                content = data[\"choices\"][0][\"message\"][\"content\"].strip()\n                facts = json.loads(content)\n                if isinstance(facts, list):\n                    for fact in facts:\n                        if isinstance(fact, dict) and \"text\" in fact:\n                            text = fact[\"text\"].strip()\n Exception:\n            pass  # Extraction is best-effort; never crash the session\n\n    async def semantic_compare(\n        self,\n        text1: str,\n        text2: str,\n        api_key: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Use gpt-5.4-nano to compare two facts semantically.\n        Returns: 'duplicate' | 'contradiction' | 'refinement' | 'distinct'\n        \"\"\"\n        prompt = (\n            \"Compare these two facts about a user. Return ONLY one word:\\n\"\n            \"- 'duplicate' if they express the same information\\n\"\n            \"- 'contradiction' if they directly contradict each other\\n\"\n            \"- 'refinement' if Fact 2 is a more specific or updated version of Fact 1\\n\"\n            \"- 'distinct' if they are unrelated or independently useful\\n\\n\"\n            f\"Fact 1: {text1}\\nFact 2: {text2}\"\n        )\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.post(\n                    \"https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions\",\n                    headers={\n                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {api_key}\",\n                        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                    },\n                    json={\n                        \"model\": \"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                        \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"refinement\", \"distinct\"):\n                    return result\n                return \"distinct\"\n        except Exception:\n            return \"distinct\"  # Fallback to safe default\n\n    def _similarity_ratio(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Calculate simple Jaccard similarity between two strings.\"\"\"\n        if not s1 or not s2:\n            return 0.0\n        words1 = set(s1.split())\n        words2 = set(s2.split())\n        intersection = len(words1 & words2)\n        union = len(words1 | words2)\n        return intersection / union if union > 0 else 0.0\n\n    def _facts_hash(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Compute a hash of current facts for summary cache invalidation.\"\"\"\n        content = json.dumps([f.to_dict() for f in self._facts], session_id: str | None = None,\n        on_backend_cost=None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return a cached one-paragraph user summary, regenerating if facts changed.\"\"\"\n        if not self._facts:\n            return \"\"\n        current_hash = self._facts_hash()\n        if (\n            self._data.get(\"summary_facts_hash\") == current_hash\n            and Focus on the most important details. \"\n            \"Write in third person (e.g., 'The user...'). \"user\", \"content\": prompt}],\n                        \"temperature\": 0.3,\n                        \"max_tokens\": 150,\n                    },\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                data = resp.json()\n                if cost_tracker is not None and session_id is not None:\n                    usage = data.get(\"usage\", {})\n  usage.get(\"completion_tokens\", 0),\n                                )\n                            except Exception:\n                                pass\n                return data[\"choices\"][0][\"message\"][\"content\"].strip()\n        except Exception:\n            return \"\"\n\n    def get_facts_by_topic(self, topic: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return facts relevant to topic using keyword substring matching.\"\"\"\n        topic_words = [w.lower() for w in topic.split() if len(w) > 3]\n        if not topic_words:\n            return []\n        matched = []\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            fact_lower = fact.text.lower()\n            if any(word in fact_lower for word in topic_words):\n                matched.append(fact.text)\n        return matched\n\n    async def get_facts_by_topic_async(self, topic: str, api_key: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Return topic-relevant facts. Uses keyword matching, falls back to embeddings.\"\"\"\n        matches = self.get_facts_by_topic(topic)\n        if matches:\n            return matches\n        return await self._get_facts_by_embedding(topic, api_key)\n\n    async def _get_facts_by_embedding(self, topic: str, api_key: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Use OpenAI text-embedding-3-small for cosine similarity retrieval.\"\"\"\n        if not self._facts:\n            return []\n        fact_texts = [f.text for f in self._facts]\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.post(\n                    \"https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings\",\n                    headers={\n                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {api_key}\",\n                        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                    },\n                    json={\n                        \"model\": \"text-embedding-3-small\",\n                        \"input\": [topic] + fact_texts,\n                    },\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                data = resp.json()\n            embeddings = [item[\"embedding\"] for item in data[\"data\"]]\n            topic_emb = embeddings[0]\n            fact_embs = embeddings[1:]\n\n            def cosine_sim(a: list, b: list) -> float:\n                dot = sum(x * y for x, y in zip(a, b))\n                norm_a = sum(x * x for x in a) ** 0.5\n                norm_b = sum(x * x for x in b) ** 0.5\n                return dot / (norm_a * norm_b) if norm_a and norm_b else 0.0\n\n            THRESHOLD = 0.4\n            scored = sorted(\n                [(cosine_sim(topic_emb, emb), text) for emb, text in zip(fact_embs, fact_texts)],\n                reverse=True,\n            )\n            return [text for score, text in scored if score >= THRESHOLD]\n        except Exception:\n            return []\n\n    async def add_fact_with_semantic_dedup(\n        self,\n        text: str,\n        api_key: str,\n        category: FactCategory = FactCategory.CONTEXT,\n        confidence: float = 0.7,\n        source: FactSource = FactSource.AUTO,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Add a fact with semantic deduplication using gpt-5.4-nano.\n        Falls back to simple dedup if API call fails.\n        Returns True if fact was added, False if duplicate/skipped.\n        \"\"\"\n        text = text.strip()\n        if not text:\n            return False\n\n        # First check simple dedup\n        if self._is_duplicate(text, category):\n            return False\n\n        # For borderline cases, use semantic comparison\n        new_normalized = self._normalize_text(text)\n        for fact in self._facts:\n            if fact.category == category:\n                existing_normalized = self._normalize_text(fact.text)\n                # If strings are somewhat similar but not exact, use semantic comparison\n                similarity = self._similarity_ratio(new_normalized, existing_normalized)\n                if 0.1 < similarity < 0.9:\n                    result = await self.semantic_compare(text, fact.text, api_key)\n                    if result == \"duplicate\":\n                        return False\n                    if result in (\"contradiction\", \"refinement\"):\n                        # Replace old fact with new (contradiction: new overrides; refinement: new is more specific)\n                        self._facts.remove(fact)\n                        break\n\n        # Trim oldest facts if at limit\n        if len(self._facts) >= MAX_FACTS:\n            self._facts = self._facts[-(MAX_FACTS - 1):]\n\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        new_fact = StructuredFact(\n            text=text,\n            category=category,\n            confidence=confidence,\n            source=source,\n            created_at=now,\n            last_referenced=now,\n        )\n        self._facts.append(new_fact)\n        self.save()\n        return True\n","numLines":602,"startLine":1,"totalLines":602}}}

Also check for JWT token in Authorization header or query parameter\n387\t    # Browser WebSockets can't send custom headers, so we fall back to query params\n388\t    auth_header = headers.get('authorization', '')\n389\t    jwt_user_info = None\n390\t    jwt_token = None\n391\t\n392\t    # Fallback: check query parameter for browser WebSocket connections\n397\t    if not jwt_token:\n398\t        try:\n399\t            from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs\n400\t            ws_url = str(websocket.url)\n401\t            if 'token=' in ws_url:\n402\t                query_params = parse_qs(urlparse(ws_url).query)\n403\t                jwt_token = query_params.get('token', [None])[0]\n404\t        except Exception as e:\n405\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse token from query params: {e}\")\n406\t\n407\t    # Retrieve and log incoming headers\n    headers = websocket.headers\n    logger = getLogger(\"uvicorn.error\")\n    logger.info(f\"Session ID for cost tracking: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Log all headers for auth proxy debugging\n    header_dict = dict(headers)\n    auth_relevant = {k: v for k, v in header_dict.items()\n                     if any(k.lower().startswith(p) for p in ('x-forward', 'x-real', 'cookie', 'authorization'))}\n    logger.info(json.dumps({\"event\": \"ws.connect\", \"session_id\": session_id,\n                            \"auth_headers\": auth_relevant, \"ts\": time.time()}))\n\n    # Also check for JWT token in Authorization header or query parameter\n    # Browser WebSockets can't send custom headers, so we fall back to query params\n    auth_header = headers.get('authorization', '')\n    jwt_user_info = None\n    jwt_token = None\n\n    # Try Authorization header first (for reverse proxy auth)\n    if auth_header and auth_header.startswith('Bearer '):\n        jwt_token = auth_header[7:]  # Remove 'Bearer ' prefix\n\n    # Fallback: check query parameter for browser WebSocket connections\n    if not jwt_token:\n        try:\n            from urllib.parse import urlparse, parse_qs\n            ws_url = str(websocket.url)\n            if 'token=' in ws_url:\n                query_params = parse_qs(urlparse(ws_url).query)\n                jwt_token = query_params.get('token', [None])[0]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse token from query params: {e}\")\n\n    # Load per-user memory (keyed by email if available, else user id)\n    memory_store: UserMemoryStore | None = None\n    if user_identity:\n        memory_store = UserMemoryStore(user_identity)\n        memory_store.load()\n\n    # Please ensure you have entries tagged with 'gpt-realtime'.\"\n            logger.error(error_msg)\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": error_msg\n            })\n            await websocket.close(code=1008, reason=\"Missing realtime model configuration\")\n            return\n\n        # Validate that config_list has at least one entry\n        if len(realtime_llm_config[\"config_list\"]) == 0:\n            error_msg = \"Configuration error: config_list is empty. Only after announcing the search \"\n            f\"should you call the web_search function.      memory_section += \".\"\n            if summary:\n                memory_section += f\"\\n{summary}\"\n            else:\n                # Fallback: list facts directly if summary unavailable\n  system_message = base_system_message\n\n        _extraction_api_key = first_config[\"api_key\"]\n\n        async def _on_turn_done(user_text: str, assistant_text: str) -> None:\n            if return\n\n    def _geocode_location(safe_location: str):\n        \"\"\"Geocode a city name to lat/lon using Open-Meteo geocoding API.\"\"\"\n        geo_response = requests.get(\n            \"https://geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search\",\n            params={\"name\": safe_location, \"count\": 1, \"language\": \"en\", \"format\": \"json\"},\n            timeout=10\n        )\n        geo_response.raise_for_status()\n        geo_data = geo_response.json()\n        results = geo_data.get(\"results\")\n        if not results:\n            return None, None, None\n        result = t_request) * 1000, 1)\n\n            # Track token usage for cost accounting\n            if hasattr(response, 'usage') and response.usage:\n                try:\n                    await cost_tracker.track_usage(\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        model=\"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                        input_tokens=response.usage.input_tokens,\n                        output_tokens=response.usage.output_tokens,\n                    )\n                except Exception:\n                    pass  # Never crash the session over cost tracking\n                try:\n                    await session.send_backend_cost(\n                        \"gpt-5.4-nano\",\n                        response.usage.input_tokens,\n                        response.usage.output_tokens,\n                    )\n                except Exception:\n                    pass\n\n            if hasattr(response, 'output_text') and response.output_text:\n                result_text = response.output_text\n                full_size = len(result_text)\n                if full_size > 2000:\n                    result_text = result_text[:2000] + \"...\"\n                logger.info(json.dumps({\"event\": \"web_search.response_received\", \"query\": safe_query,\n                                        \"full_size\": full_size,\n                                        \"result_size\": len(result_text),\n                                        \"duration_ms\": duration_ms,\n                                        \"ts\": time.time()}))\n                return result_text\n            else:\n                logger.warning(json.dumps({\"event\": \"web_search.empty_response\", \"query\": safe_query,\n                                           \"duration_ms\": duration_ms,\n                                           \"ts\": time.time()}))\n                return \"I couldn't find relevant information. Please try a different query.\"\n\n        except ValidationError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Search query validation failed for '{query}': {e}\")\n            return \"Invalid search query. Please rephrase your question.\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Web search error: {str(e)}\", exc_info=True)\n            return \"I'm having trouble searching the web right now. Please try again.\"\n\n    session.register_tool(\n        name=\"web_search\",\n        description=\"Search the web for current information, recent news, or specific topics using OpenAI's web search.
